activities_projects.title: Imagery Analysis Chart
activities_projects.objective: Analyze and interpret imagery in 'Dust of Snow' and 'Fire and Ice'.
steps: Select lines from both poems that create strong visual images
Describe the images and explain their impact on mood
Create a chart showing imagery and corresponding emotions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand how imagery conveys emotions and themes.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of analysis, accurate examples, and relevance

activities_projects.title: Theme Exploration Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Identify and discuss themes in both poems.
steps: List themes such as change, human desire, and destructive emotions
Provide textual evidence supporting each theme
Discuss with peers how themes are expressed differently in each poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance comprehension and critical discussion skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of themes and textual support

activities_projects.title: Mood and Tone Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Track the shift in mood and tone across both poems.
steps: Identify the starting and ending mood of each poem
Highlight tone words used by Frost
Draw a visual map connecting lines with tone and mood changes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop analytical skills to interpret tone and mood.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of mapping and textual connections

activities_projects.title: Symbolism Interpretation
activities_projects.objective: Analyze symbols and their deeper meanings.
steps: List symbols (crow, hemlock, fire, ice)
Explain what each symbol represents in context
Write a short paragraph connecting symbols to the poem’s message
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will strengthen literary interpretation skills.

activities_projects.title: Comparative Analysis Essay
activities_projects.objective: Compare poetic techniques between the two poems.
steps: Identify techniques such as rhyme, imagery, and conciseness
Explain how each technique enhances meaning in the poem
Write a structured essay comparing the poems’ techniques
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will improve analytical and essay writing skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, textual evidence, and comparative insight

activities_projects.title: Creative Poem Writing
activities_projects.objective: Compose a poem inspired by Frost’s style and theme.
steps: Select a natural incident or event that changed your mood
Write a short poem using imagery and symbolism
Share the poem with the class for feedback
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will apply poetic techniques creatively.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, relevance, and use of poetic devices

activities_projects.title: Character/Emotion Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Explore emotions expressed in the poems.
steps: Identify emotions such as regret, hope, desire, and hate
Associate each emotion with lines from the poems
Draw a visual representation of emotional flow
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand emotional impact and character perspective.

activities_projects.title: Poem Illustration Poster
steps: Select a key theme from each poem
Create drawings or symbols representing these themes
Present the poster and explain your choices
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will integrate visual creativity with literary understanding.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, clarity, and relevance to the poem

activities_projects.title: Line-by-Line Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Deepen understanding through detailed textual analysis.
steps: Choose a stanza from each poem
Explain word choice, imagery, and rhythm in your own words
Discuss how these elements contribute to the overall message
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop critical reading and interpretive skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and textual support

activities_projects.title: Poetic Message Debate
activities_projects.objective: Discuss and defend interpretations of Frost’s message.
steps: Identify what you think Frost’s main message is in each poem
Prepare arguments with textual evidence
Engage in a classroom debate presenting and defending your view
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance reasoning, argumentation, and literary interpretation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of arguments, use of text, and presentation skills
